additional_benefits: Cytokinin-rich foliage supports plant growth in companion systems
warnings: Lopping should be regular to prevent excessive shading.
Use boundary planting where root spread will not compete severely with the main crop.
Leaves and young pods are edible, but old fibrous biomass is less suitable for food use.
primary_source: Natural farming and agroforestry recommendations for Moringa oleifera as a boundary mulch tree

planting_density: Boundary or block planting; fast-growing tree reaching about 3 m in 90 days
additional_benefits: Rapid biomass production for green mulch
Nitrogen becomes available about 2-3 weeks after incorporation
Useful for quick canopy and fodder biomass in mixed systems
warnings: Do not over-lop very young plants before establishment.
Allow sufficient recovery between harvests to sustain biomass production.
Best performance requires good drainage and active growth conditions.
primary_source: Sesbania grandiflora natural farming and green manure guidance

planting_season: Anytime as cuttings; best during onset of rains
planting_density: Directly plant 30 cm cuttings into soil; maintain as boundary hedge
additional_benefits: Fresh leaves are a strong termite repellent in mulch systems
Excellent hedge biomass for repeated lopping
Useful as live fence and green manure source
warnings: Fresh leaves are toxic to dogs, cats, and livestock if eaten in quantity.
Do not feed fresh material directly to animals.
Use well-managed lopping to avoid excessive competition with nearby crops.
primary_source: Gliricidia sepium agroforestry and natural farming lopping protocols

additional_benefits: Deep taproot helps break hardpan and improve soil structure
warnings: Incorporate at flowering stage around day 45-50 for best green manure value.
Do not allow seed set if the crop is meant for soil incorporation.
Requires timely incorporation so biomass does not become too fibrous.
primary_source: Green manure recommendations for Crotalaria juncea in tropical farming systems

additional_benefits: Ideal green manure before paddy transplanting
Fast biomass production in short cycle
warnings: Incorporate before paddy transplanting so biomass decomposes in time.
Best suited for wet fields and lowland rice systems.
Do not let the crop become too woody before incorporation.
primary_source: Sesbania bispinosa green manure guidance for lowland rice systems
